calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-24-2023 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-20-2023 Continued To Apr-24-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-23-2023 Continued To Mar-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Benjamin Bernard, Esq. and Katelyn Phillips, Esq. for the San Francisco Public Guardain (SFPG) (Petitioner/Trustee/Conservator).

The Court stated that it appreciated everyone's hard work, that the supplement that was filed helped tie things up for the Court, that it does not like paragraph seven of the trust, that it thinks that it is an error or a mistake, that this has been analyzed by counsel, that it appears that an amendment to the petition is needed, and that notice should be given since the amounts will change. Ms. Phillips stated that they agree with the Court, noted that paragraph seven is confusing, and confirmed that they will prepare an amended petition. The Court stated that it appears that counsel understands what the Court is trying to accomplish.

The First and Final Account and Report of Successor Trustee; Petition for Settlement and Approval Thereof; for Confirmation of Proposed Distribution to Beneficiaries; and for Allowance and Approval of Trustee Fees and Attorney Fees is continued to March 20, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-12-2022 Continued To Jan-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-07-2022 Off Calendar

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Aug-01-2022 Continued To Nov-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-28-2022 Continued To Aug-01-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-29-2021 Continued To Mar-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Benjamin Bernard, via ZOOM, for the San Francisco Public Guardian ("SFPG").

Counsel reports of the difficulties with this matter including the 30 beneficiaries 20 of which live in Switzerland. Counsel advises that the plan is to make distributions to the beneficiaries in Switzerland via wire transfer instead of being mailed and asks for a four-month continuance to confirm the preceding. The court recognizes the difficulties with this case but advises counsel that a continuance of that magnitude will require the SFPG to begin working on this matter immediately.

The Future Accounting is continued to Mar-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
